title: An ~Honest~ Review of A Top School - Emory University Review 2019

Need answers to the biggest questions about Emory? --> https://youtu.be/6dqsEXNpKIA
author: @hello-sjc

title: An ~Honest~ Review of A Top School - Emory University Review 2019
body:
Thanks for your review, it was honest and awesome! I am from the south (a smaller Georgia city) so disagree about the Atlanta thing (most southern cities are just sprawly and suburban. Atlanta technically has a more urban culture than the other big cities despite being sprawly. But nonetheless, I did see people stay in the Emory bubble all 4 years and thought why?). The funding thing is definitely true, but unfortunately that is the case at a lot of even "elite" universities. The disappointing thing is that Emory is better than a lot of elite schools in terms of the buildings and funding outside (funding is leaving those areas) of sciences and its major professional schools (like GBS), and research so it is clearing a low bar. But yeah, it is a problem. The chair.....I've seen that type of stuff happen. Rough. And I always thought space usage was always a problem. You have Emory, which is more academically oriented than some of its say, "more social" (basically smart party schools. I didn't want a place trying to emulate like a classical party school, so Emory was great for me. You can make the social scene fit whatever interest and personality you had there) peers and it builds a beautiful building like the chem building, and claims it is supposed to promote collaborative learning in open spaces, yet the location of outlets don't make much sense and are often located from distal areas. I need not even talk about the old DUC.

And 4/5 isn't bad depending on how hard the course is lol. Some things are curve graded especially in the sciences, but based on your critiques (which I agree with for the most part), a lot of elite schools would get 3.5-4/5 because none of them are perfect and have similar or worse problems that nobody really talks about perhaps to preserve the appearance of prestige and perfection at the school. Generally Emory (kind of like Harvard) students are more honest and do tell what they want done and complain a lot. I personally recommend finding the right administrators to contact about certain issues and make a legit, evidence based (and anecdotal, because story telling is great and you did a great job of that here) case to them if possible. Trust me, it works more than you would think. I've done it and like a year or 2 later, some form of improvement in my areas of concern would happen likely because I wasn't the only one thinking about them. You (alum) and current students have WAY more say so than you think. You just have to complain to the right people. You can't just accept stuff. But it is always great to say that Emory has never been a school where students turn blind eyes and just pretend it is perfect so that it can retain popularity and a higher rank or something.
author: @2008bscott

title: An ~Honest~ Review of A Top School - Emory University Review 2019

title: An ~Honest~ Review of A Top School - Emory University Review 2019
body:
I have to say as a long term alum, I enjoyed my time at Emory. However, I believe the biggest issue with Emory was a lack of school spirit, community, and alumni networking. After I left Emory, I actually felt closer ties to my professional school because of this.
I'm just wondering, as I have a child thinking of applying, has this improved? Do students feel truly connected to the university or does it feel more like a pre-professional school than anything else?

title: 73 Questions With An Emory Student | A Human Health Major
body: Everything he said was true. Facts.  Including the diversity part. And even though the buildings  give you that large classic feel. Im always like were are the students. They are there but its not as many as you would think.  Its almost marketed as a larger school than what it is. I been to one dining hall and it did have a lot of choices. But nothing amazing. The campus is nice. But when he said Pharmacy he probaly made a good choice its about finishing at the end of the day. They do have a barnes and nobles on their campus. And a few outside food spots close by.. And carlos museaum is small but it does house some egyptian artifacts. I've never been to the library but all the buildings seem to be nice inside. Great video!

title: Spilling the Tea on Emory University | Unfiltered Q&A with Students!
body: If y'all aren't from Georgia, make friends with Georgians and it will likely improve the range of options for "spontaneous" adventures to clubs or social events on more boring nights, because people from GSU, Georgia Tech, and the AUC have a lot more access and Georgians are more likely to know those people and can pull you into that as an alternative to the typical Emory scene.  It's just really fun.

title: EXPOSING Georgia Tech… #georgiatech #college #collegelife #university
body: It's amazing you found ANYONE from georgia at georgia tech. Only 50% of entering freshman are from georgia. What's it like at FSU? Nobody from Florida there?  Tech is an INTERNATIONAL university. At least 30% are foreign. Its the worlds leading computer university. Computer students are NORTORIOUSLY fussy, and like MOST nerds, LOVE to make YOUTUBE videos,....OOPS! The VAST majority of students, INCLUDING grad students are NOT from georgia. Only 32.5% of student body is non hispanic white. TBS, TNT, TCM, Adult Swim, and CNN studios are the campus bookends of Techwood drive. The school was the 1996 Olympic village. The library was used as Google headquarters in a movie. The campus has movies filmed there. There are at least a dozen world headquarters adjacent to campus, including coca cola, ncr, and Norfolk Southern. It is a very cozmo campus. Much of off campus housing are luxurious high rises with rooftop pools. I don't doubt that your unique appearance may have startled folks in Atlangelos! The symphony, art museum, and dozens of skyscrapers up to 1000 feet tall are less than 3 blocks from the dorms. It's on the MARTA line AND the IKEA store is on the west side of campus at Atlantic Station.
author: @JohnSmith-dk4qe

title: NURSING SCHOOL WEEK IN THE LIFE | a rough week, mental breakdown, 3 clinicals, exam
body: Girl tell me about it, I started college in summer of 23 after I graduated high school and I took my CNA class cause we have to be a CNA to be admitted to the program, taking 5 classes in the fall for gen eds, then more in the spring and more over summer, then i got accepted into the nursing program so I was doing nursing and two accelerated classes and now im in my 2/4 semester of nursing school taking 2 more gen eds. It's rough. I'm getting my ADN and it's a 2-year program and it's quite a lot. But, we will do great :D

title: NURSING SCHOOL WEEK IN THE LIFE | a rough week, mental breakdown, 3 clinicals, exam
body:
I’ve been a nurse for almost 10 years now… but I remember the struggle of just being overloaded with exams every week. They probably do this to help get you prepared for your nursing career.
When I was a new nurse, I was exhausted. You need to figure out time management, working your “3” days a week and then coming home exhausted and going back again.
It does get better!

title: Day in my life as a Sterile Processing Technician #spd #sterileprocessing #surgery #medical
body: I'm a retired  Cna, an a Retired Mental health Lpn. I did my research  an I'm gonna go back to work and take the class for Sterile Processing  tech, I was trained for this back in the 1980's, but I didn't  receive  a certificate.  So now I can take the course @ my community College, an I like that fact that in this position you're  working in a introverted position.   Even though I like working  with patients,  the physical demand of working with patients  can really be physically  demanding.
author: @ameliaalexander6771

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: I’ve been doing this for over 30 yrs and I still love it, great income, challenges left and right with a great staff and super chill Surgeons, I highly recommend this career
author: @lt.spears1889

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: omg thank you for spraying down the instrumentation ! You do not know how many surgical techs that does not pre clean instruments which makes our job a bit harder 😫 -SPD tech here ❤
author: @1LifeWithTink

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: I was accepted into the surgical tech program at a community college. I knew that along time ago the field was predominantly male but now it’s the opposite.I attended skills lab and of course I’m the only male and also I’m the oldest (37).  I didn’t really think that either my age or my gender would’ve mattered but it did. Even my lab instructors were different towards me. After my experience, I considered changing my major but I’ve thought, prayed and decided to not allow that bad experience to discourage me from doing what I love.I have found another school that offers the surgical tech program and am looking forward to graduation.
author: @stephenprice3357

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: Shout out to the surg techs that spray down the instruments it’s makes a huge difference!  (SPD tech) looking into surgical tech programs, but I’m super hesitant because allot of people say the surgeons are disrespectful and it’s stressful.
author: @Lydia-jy1bh

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: I have been dreaming about being a surgical tech since 2015. I’ve been a professional coder since 2009 and have always been fascinated with surgeries and would watch the surgeries to further understand the coding scenarios. After being a professional multi specialty coder and auditor for the last 15 years I’ve made the decision to go back to school and get my surgical tech degree and get into the OR. Working from home over the last decade has been rewarding but now that my kids are getting older, I’m ready to get out of my home office and into the OR.
author: @southerncharmcreative

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: I’m currently a sterile processing technician and I’d like to move on to a surgical technician!  Sterile processing is overwhelming! Overworked and underpaid! Plus the surg techs make more traveling! From my experience as a traveler! But thank you for spraying the instruments down it helps us out so much ❤

body: New Subscriber here!’ I’m an aspiring tech, I’m already in school for it. My mother was a tech and it was completely different back in her day. I look forward to seeing more from you 🥰
author: @keionnasublett521

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM
body: Scheduled to start my program in October but as of recent I've been having more and more questions come to mind. I talked to a nurse whom I work with at the current hospital I'm employed with and she told me that it wasn't worth it. Quote "There's no room for career growth and all you do is stand there and retract. Besides that you don't really do anything. Not only that but most of the cases will be probably the same, time and time again and the doctors and residents can act really childish"  Hearing that made me kinda bummed out especially since I was really looking forward to getting started, but if being a CST is just standing around, holding organs back, and giving doc or the first assistant instruments it sounds hella boring. I thought as a CST I would be more involved with the cases and have atleast some patient interaction. If this isn't the case can you please put your two sense.
author: @MirrorMaskVRDJ

title: SURGICAL TECHNOLOGIST DAY IN THE LIFE | DAY IN MY LIFE | OPERATING ROOM

body: Rasiya, watching your journey brought tears to my eyes. I remember Anjali telling me how thoughtful and grounded you are, and it all came through in this video. The way you handled both the heartbreak and the joy with such grace is beyond your years. Vanderbilt is lucky to have you—not just for your talent, but for your heart and spirit. Congratulations on this beautiful new chapter!

title: COLLEGE DECISION REACTIONS (EMOTIONAL) | Likely Letter, Ivies, Stanford, T20s, & More
body: It’s my first time to comment on these kinds of content. But I am happy & proud that you made the right decision to take the full-ride! I think amongst all students that I’ve watched on these vids, you’re the only one (so far) who chose the college who offered you a FULL-RIDE rather than the Ivy leagues which are far more expensive. After you graduate, you will see the real world and I’m sure that you will always be grateful and proud of yourself that you are debt-free at a young age. You are amazing and i’m so happy that you helped your parents as well. Goodluck and enjoy college!😊
